summary.intent: Add a live Expo web app and local Express+WebSocket API server to the gossamer claude-hook-handler project, enabling real-time viewing of active Claude sessions and their events. Then fix table column alignment and improve formatting of user and keywords columns.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented the backend server (src/server.ts), serve subcommand, and complete Expo app with navigation, API client, screens, and components. The app served successfully at http://localhost:19006. Column alignment was fixed by exporting shared COL_WIDTHS constants, user column renders as a mailto link, and keywords render as chips.

repo: Project uses NodeNext module resolution — all local imports in src/ must use .js extensions
Two separate tsconfigs must coexist: root (NodeNext) and app/ (expo/tsconfig.base) — they must not be merged
Dynamic import of server.ts in handler.ts is intentional to avoid loading express/ws on every hook invocation
expandHome() is called in handler.ts before passing dbPath to startServer, so server.ts always receives an absolute path
WebSocket change detection uses polling (db.event.findFirst orderBy id desc every 2s) rather than DB triggers

code.finding: Exporting COL_WIDTHS from SessionRow and importing it into ActiveSessions header is the pattern to keep header and row columns aligned when mixing fixed-width and flex layouts
WebSocket subscribeToUpdates uses exponential backoff reconnect: 1s → 2s → ... → 30s cap; returns an unsubscribe fn that closes WS and cancels reconnect timer
WebSocketServer({ server: httpServer }) shares port 3000 with Express — no separate WS port needed. SIGINT/SIGTERM handler clears the poller interval before closing

workflow: npm install in a subdirectory (app/) can fail when the parent node_modules hoists a partial react package, causing version to appear as 'undefined' — use --legacy-peer-deps to bypass
@expo/metro-runtime must be explicitly installed for Expo web support; it is not pulled in automatically
Single port for HTTP + WebSocket is achievable by passing the http.Server instance to WebSocketServer constructor
Testing a serve command quickly: start on an alternate port, curl the API, kill the process — avoids conflicts with already-running instances

friction: react@18.3.2 specified in plan does not exist on npm; Expo 52 requires react@18.3.1
react-native@0.76.3 specified in plan is wrong for Expo 52; correct version is 0.76.9
@expo/metro-runtime was not listed in the plan's dependencies but is required for Expo web
npm install in app/ conflicted with parent node_modules hoisting react as version 'undefined', requiring --legacy-peer-deps
Port 3000 EADDRINUSE during smoke-testing because a prior test server process was still running
Initial ActiveSessions header used hardcoded pixel widths while rows used flex, causing column misalignment that required a follow-up fix
open_items: COL_WIDTHS are hardcoded pixel values — may need responsive adjustment for narrow viewports
Keywords chips in SessionRow clip on overflow with no 'show more' affordance
mailto: link for user column only works in environments with a mail client configured
2-second DB polling for WebSocket change detection is a simple approach; could be replaced with SQLite WAL hooks or file watching for lower latency
API server has no authentication (cors with no origin restriction) — acceptable for local dev tool but worth noting if ever exposed
app/ node_modules are separate from root — contributors must run npm install in both locations; no workspace setup
